back_translation: And I looked, and behold, the Lamb was standing on Mount Zion, and with Him there were one-hundred-forty-four thousand people who had His name and His Father's name written on their foreheads
And I heard a voice from heaven, like the voice of many waters, and like the voice of great thunder. And the voice that I heard was like the voice of harpists playing harps

And they sang a new song before the throne, before the four living-creatures, and before the elders. And no one could learn that song, except the one-hundred-forty-four thousand people, those who had been redeemed from the earth

These people are those who have not defiled themselves with women, because they are virgins. These people are those who follow the Lamb wherever He goes. These people have been redeemed from among humanity, as firstfruits for God and for the Lamb
And in their mouths no lie was found. They are blameless
And I saw another angel flying in the middle of the sky, having an eternal gospel to proclaim to those dwelling on the earth, and to every nation, every tribe, every tongue, and every people
He said with a loud voice, ‘Fear God and give glory to Him, because the hour of His judgment has come. And worship Him who created the heavens, the earth, the sea, and all the springs of water’
And the second angel followed, saying, ‘Babylon the great has fallen, has fallen, the one who has caused all nations to drink the wine of the violence of her sexual-immorality’
And the third angel followed them, saying with a loud voice, ‘If anyone worships the wild-beast and its image, and receives the mark on the forehead or on the hand

That one will drink the wine of the wrath of God, poured undiluted into the cup of His wrath. And he will be tormented in fire and sulfur in the presence of the holy angels, and in the presence of the Lamb
And the smoke of the torment of them ascends forever and ever. And they have no rest day or night, namely those who worship the wild-beast and its image, and whoever receives the mark of its name’
Here is the perseverance of the saints, namely those who keep the commandments of God and faith in Jesus

And I heard a voice from heaven saying, ‘Write: Blessed are the dead, those who die in the Lord from now on.’ ‘Yes,’ the Spirit says, ‘so that they will rest from their labors, because their deeds will follow them’
And I looked, and behold, there was a white cloud, and One like the Son of Man was seated on that cloud, wearing a golden crown on His head, and in His hand there was a sharp sickle

And another angel came out from the temple, calling with a loud voice to the One seated on the cloud, ‘Please swing Your sickle down and reap, because the time of harvest has come, because the crop of the earth is ripe’
And the One seated on the cloud swung His sickle on the earth, and the earth was harvested
And another angel came out from the temple in heaven, having a sharp sickle as well

And another angel came out from the altar, having authority over fire, called with a loud voice to the angel who held the sharp sickle, ‘Swing your sharp sickle, and gather the clusters of grapes of the vine of the earth, because its grapes are ripe’
And that angel swung his sickle on the earth, and gathered the vine of the earth, and threw it into the great winepress of the wrath of God
And the winepress was trampled outside the city, and blood flowed out from that pit, as high as the horses' bridles, for a distance of one-thousand-six-hundred stadia
